body:
## Purpose 
Update how we handle evaporation when the bucket is not saturated: E = beta*E_0(q_sat)

The current code relies on the snow cover fraction being either 0 or 1. Snow and soil covered surfaces have the same surface temperature and roughness lengths, in the model, but the specific humidity at the surface changes depending on the surface coverage. If the snow cover fraction is 0 or 1, we can store a single value q_sfc in `p`, which is computed as the saturation specific humidity over ice or liquid water depending on if SCF is 0 or 1.

Similarly, we have an effective beta = (1-sigma)*beta + sigma*1.

Evaporation is the computed with beta_eff and the single value of q_sfc.

If the snow cover fraction was not 1, we would need to separately store the surface q over both snow and soil, and compute two sets of fluxes, using different values of beta.

Closes #123 

## To-do

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

----
- [x] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: update beta factor for evaporation
